description: Returning errors in varied shapes across endpoints forces clients into brittle parsing and can leak internal details.
body: Failure mode: different endpoints return errors as plain strings, varied JSON shapes, or raw stack traces because no envelope was defined. Detection signals: clients special-case error parsing per endpoint; some errors expose internal messages or stack frames; there is no stable error code to branch on. Do not let each endpoint invent its own error format. Safer fallback: adopt one error envelope with a stable machine-readable code, message, and details across the surface, map status codes consistently, and keep messages free of sensitive internals.
